content.name: Edit
content.input: [object Object]{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/Users/krestnikov/giga/compression-drives-truth/paper_neurips/main.tex", "old_string": "\\textbf{Model scale.}\\quad The primary experiments use models of 3.5M--86M parameters. Section~\\ref{sec:qwen1b} extends to a Qwen3-architecture model (${\\sim}$1B parameters) trained on real text, confirming the random/coherent contrast at larger scale. However, this remains far below the scale of production LLMs (7B--400B+), and the 1B result uses only a single seed per condition. For coherent errors, an MDL argument suggests the result should hold regardless of scale: equally compressible systems at equal frequency offer no basis for preference. Replication at multi-billion scale with compute-matched training remains an important next step.", "new_string": "\\textbf{Model scale.}\\quad The primary experiments use models of 3.5M--86M parameters. Section~\\ref{sec:qwen1b} extends to ${\\sim}$1B parameters on a mixed natural-text corpus with a single seed per condition, providing a scale-up sanity check but not a definitive large-scale confirmation. This remains far below production LLMs (7B--400B+). Replication at multi-billion scale with compute-matched training and multiple seeds remains an important next step."}
